props.content: Install the binary first, then your preferred SDK:

curl -sL https://web.b4n1.com/install | bash

pip install b4n1-web          # Python
npm install b4n1-web           # JavaScript
go get github.com/B4N1-com/b4n1-web/go   # Go
dotnet add package B4n1Web     # C#
# Java: see Maven section below
Three modes for different use cases:

Light   — HTTP fetch + HTML parsing (~15MB, instant)
JS      — Light + JavaScript tag extraction
Render  — Full Chromium + screenshots (~100MB)

from b4n1web import AgentBrowser, BrowserMode

browser = AgentBrowser(mode=BrowserMode.LIGHT)
page = browser.goto("https://example.com")
print(page.markdown)
print(f"Found {len(page.links)} links")

# Render mode with wait-for
browser.mode = BrowserMode.RENDER
page = browser.goto("https://example.com", wait_for="#search-results")
browser.close()
All SDKs return the same structured data:

url         — Final URL after navigation
markdown    — Page content as markdown text
links       — List of all links found on page
screenshot  — Base64 PNG data (render mode only)

page.getMainContent()         — Content without headers
page.findLinksByText("more")  — Links containing text
Render mode captures screenshots as base64 PNG. Decode to file:

b4n1web goto https://example.com --mode render \
  | grep "Screenshot:" \
  | sed 's/Screenshot: //' \
  | base64 -d > screenshot.png
Available tools: goto(url, mode, wait_for) — Navigate and extract content, get_links() — Get all links from current page

b4n1web goto https://example.com --mode light
b4n1web goto https://example.com --mode render --wait-for "#content"
b4n1web mcp -p 8765
b4n1web install cursor        # Configure for Cursor AI
b4n1web install claude-code   # Configure for Claude
b4n1web chromium install
